title: T-060 — UI: TEC-085 factory_specs Review modal + TEC-086 Factory Specs List
prompt: Build TEC-085 factory_specs review modal and TEC-086 factory_specs list per §5.2A/§10A using Technical-owned `factory_specs`/`internal_product_spec` as the data source, not generic `reference_tables.specifications`. Match referenced Technical prototype surfaces while applying shared release adapter T-081, RM usability blockers and Technical approver workflow: list -> open factory_spec -> review paired BOM/release blockers -> approve_for_factory or reject through T-080/T-079 APIs. RED tests/ui/tec-085-086-factory-specs.test.tsx. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-085-086-factory-specs.test.tsx`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Cross-item factory_specs/specifications list + Technical review modal for approve-for-factory sign-off.
pipeline_inputs.details: UI reads/writes Technical-owned factory_specs/internal_product_spec records. Do not use generic reference_tables.specifications as the backing store. Show shared release status, paired BOM status, RM usability blockers, clone-on-write warning for released edits, and read-only Quality lab status when present.
acceptance_criteria: Given list and modal render, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:460-483 and design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:74-108, then list columns and modal field set match — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL.
Given a Technical approver clicks Approve for factory, when API returns 200, then modal closes, shared release status changes to approved_for_factory/released, and audit evidence is visible.
Given user lacks technical.spec.approve or technical.factory_spec.approve, when on page, then approve/sign-off actions are disabled with a Technical permission explanation.
Given RM usability or release blockers exist, when modal opens, then approve is disabled and each blocker code/remediation from validateRmUsability/shared release guard is shown.

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
Given list queries run, then the backing domain is Technical `factory_specs`/`internal_product_spec`, not generic `reference_tables.specifications`.
Given factory_spec has paired BOM pending approval, UI links to T-090 bundle panel and never claims NPD G4 alone unlocks factory use.
test_strategy: RED tests for parity + RBAC + signoff flow.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not bypass audit on signoff.
Do not label the approver as Quality Lead; Technical owns factory_spec approval.
Do not hide release blockers behind generic validation errors.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Authorization helper/preflight used to enable/disable approval actions.
